$comment: Diffract universal tool registry. Each entry defines a third-party CLI the agent can use, across four layers. INSTALL fields (repo/ref/kind/patch/build/entry/bin) are consumed at image-build time by install-diffract-tools.sh to bake the tool into /sandbox/.diffract-tools/<name> and put <bin> on the agent PATH. CONNECT fields (secretEnv/configEnv/apiHosts/binaries/authHeader) are consumed at runtime by diffract-tool-connect.sh to wire the tool's credentials securely (OpenShell generic provider -> placeholder in sandbox -> real secret injected at egress; egress allowlisted to apiHosts, attributed to binaries). ADVERTISE fields (the optional 'skill' object: name/title/summary/tags/examples) are consumed at image-build time by advertise-diffract-tools.sh to emit a hermes SKILL.md under /sandbox/.hermes/skills/diffract-tools/<name>, so the agent DISCOVERS the tool in its skill catalog and knows it is pre-authenticated (install/connect make a tool runnable; advertise makes the agent aware of it). 'patch' is an optional shell command run after clone and before build, in the tool dir — use it for proxy-compat fixups (the sandbox routes egress through an MITM proxy; node tools must use undici/fetch, so an axios tool needs adapter:'fetch'). Add a tool = add an entry here; no Dockerfile edit.

tools.name: facebook
tools.description: Facebook & Instagram Graph API — read and reply to Page/Instagram comments and send DMs. Pre-authenticated via the operator's Facebook login; install-less (the agent calls the Graph API with curl).

skill.name: facebook
skill.title: Facebook & Instagram (Graph API)
skill.summary: Read and respond to Facebook Page and Instagram comments and direct messages. Pre-authenticated: a Facebook Page access token is injected at the network layer. Call the Graph API with curl — list/reply to comments, send Messenger and Instagram DMs.
examples: # The token in $FACEBOOK_PAGE_TOKEN is an injected placeholder — use it verbatim, never print it,
# and never ask the user for it. It is a Facebook PAGE token. Always use curl (egress is allowed
# only for curl). Resource IDs (page/post/comment/media) go in the URL PATH — discover them from
# the calls below rather than guessing.
# STEP 1 — identify your page (a page token's /me IS the page):
curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $FACEBOOK_PAGE_TOKEN" "https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0/<COMMENT_ID>/comments" --data-urlencode "message=Thanks for reaching out!"
# List recent conversations (each has a participant PSID):
# Reply to a user who messaged the page (24h window; messaging_type=RESPONSE):

curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $FACEBOOK_PAGE_TOKEN" "https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0/me/messages" --data-urlencode 'recipient={"id":"<PSID>"}' --data-urlencode 'messaging_type=RESPONSE' --data-urlencode 'message={"text":"Hi! How can we help?"}'
# --- INSTAGRAM (Business/Creator account linked to the page) ---
# Get the linked IG account id + username:
# List comments on an IG media:
# Send an Instagram direct message (recipient id from a conversation/webhook):
